items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown
items.summary: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

items.title: Alex de Minaur loses to Hamad Medjedovic at Barcelona Open
items.summary: Alex de Minaur's uneven season has hit another road bump as Australia's number one succumbed to a shock last-16 loss at the Barcelona Open.

items.title: Pop culture quiz: Can you tell the difference between The Pitt and ER?
items.summary: Ahead of The Pitt season finale dropping this week, can you pick the difference between it and its hospital drama ancestor ER?

items.title: How these queer couples divide the mental load in their household
items.summary: After publishing our story on the four stages of the mental load, we asked to hear from queer couples about how they divide and conquer the cognitive and emotional labour at home.

items.title: Love story: When love means donating a kidney to your brother
items.summary: Despite a 10-year age gap and geographical separation, Sajarn and Codey Stow have retained a close bond since childhood. That bond has only strengthened since Codey received a donor kidney from his brother last November.

items.title: Cameron Young, Jason Day apply pressure to Rory McIlroy in third round of Masters
items.summary: It will not be a procession to a second straight Masters title for Rory McIlroy, after a tense third round at Augusta National.

items.title: Hard Quiz: Still trying to prove you're good at trivia? You're not fooling me
items.summary: I applaud your courage for having a go at this quiz. That's about it, though.

items.title: Australia vs Great Britain Billie Jean King Cup: Talia Gibson and Kimberly Birrell lose singles
items.summary: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

items.title: Truck driver charged over Sunshine Coast crash that killed three-year-old girl
items.summary: A truck driver has been charged with drug driving over a crash that killed a three-year-old girl and critically injured her one-year-old sister.

items.title: Nitrous oxide a 'significant contributing factor' in Canberra teenager's drowning death, coroner finds
items.summary: An 18-year-old was heavily impaired by nitrous oxide, known as "nangs", when his car entered a pond, prompting a coroner to call for a review of the gas's availability in the ACT.

items.title: Regular unleaded petrol to peak in May — as it happened
items.summary: Westpac has drawn a line in the sand and said unleaded petrol prices are projected to peak at $2.46 a litre in late May, with a 26 cents increase when excise returns to normal. Look back at how the trading session unfolded with our specialist business reporters.

items.title: Nick Faldo says he will not comment on rival Greg Norman until after US Masters
items.summary: Respectfully biting his tongue, Nick Faldo is refusing to dignify fresh criticism from Greg Norman that is certain to reignite one of golf's fiercest rivalries.

items.title: 'Ketamine Queen' jailed for 15 years over death of Friends star Matthew Perry
items.summary: Jayvee Sangha admits to running a "stash house" that helped supply the famous Friends star ketamine in the months before his death.

items.title: Travel quiz, April 8: Pack your suitcase, we're going on a trip
items.summary: Which European microstate is smaller? And which US president isn’t on Mount Rushmore?

items.title: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner facing drink driving charges in Sydney
items.summary: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner is charged with mid-range drink driving in Sydney's east over the weekend.

items.title: Jason Day sad to see Tiger Woods struggle with addiction, miss Masters after DUI charge
items.summary: Australian number two Jason Day says Tiger Woods's struggles are a reminder that anyone can struggle with addiction, but admits it was "selfish" for the golf great to get behind the wheel.

items.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland coast
items.summary: The Bureau of Meteorology is increasingly confident the system will move towards the Queensland coast later in the week, potentially crossing Cape York Peninsula.

items.title: Hard Quiz: Hurry up, I don't have all day to watch you struggle at internet trivia
items.summary: It's painfully obvious you're going to fail this quiz, so let's get it over with and return to our Sunday mornings, shall we?

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of Top Gun, bowling underarm and your weight on the Moon
items.summary: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: England's Harry Brook and Jacob Bethell let off with warning over New Zealand nightclub altercation
items.summary: Harry Brook and Jacob Bethell escape with a caution notice following an investigation into a nightclub incident in ‌New Zealand late ⁠last year.

items.title: Australians reveal toll of fuel price rises on their lives as cost of living goes up
items.summary: The ABC asked how you were coping with fuel prices and here's what we heard back.

items.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi half-century leads Rajasthan Royals to IPL win over Chennai Super Kings
items.summary: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashes a 15-ball half-century as he leads Rajasthan Royals to an easy victory over Chennai Super Kings.

items.title: More than 200 buildings damaged in Exmouth, residents stranded as airport, roads remain closed
items.summary: Visitors in Exmouth who were stuck there when Cyclone Narelle passed are now trying to find ways out of the town, but that is proving impossible for one grieving family.

items.title: Australia takes 2-0 lead in three-game ODI series against West Indies at Warner Park
items.summary: Australia takes an unassailable 2-0 lead over the West Indies with a cruisy 91-run victory in the second ODI at St Kitts' Warner Park.

items.title: WA tourist towns grapple with wildlife loss, damage to businesses after Cyclone Narelle
items.summary: In the popular tourist destination, it appears the category four cyclone has chewed up and spat out whole sections of the Ningaloo Reef onto dry land.

items.title: State library showcases photo archive of trailblazing Indigenous journalist Wayne Coolwell
items.summary: Journalist and broadcaster Wayne Coolwell documented years of Indigenous culture with his 35mm camera. The photographs are going on showcase at the State Library.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Mannix starts Mums WhatsApp group, Wallam's revenge and Housby back to her best
items.summary: Emily Mannix talks about her extended absence from the Super Netball league, and why she's now ready to return for the Melbourne Vixens after a "freak accident" meant her daughter Mabel needed emergency care at birth.

items.title: Illegal e-bike riders fined in Sydney's south as police warn of further enforcement
items.summary: More than 300 e-bike riders have been stopped, including e-scooter riders, during an ongoing police operation in the Sutherland Shire.

items.title: Donald Trump orders ICE agents to man US airport security checkpoints
items.summary: A fight over funding for the Department of Homeland Security leads to lengthy queues at US airports.

items.title: The ABC is investigating aged care again and we want to hear from you
items.summary: We're launching an investigation asking you — staff, insiders, those navigating the aged care system and their supporters — to help the ABC understand if the government's promise of reform has been delivered.

items.title: China cracks down on illegal fentanyl trade after Trump delays Beijing meeting
items.summary: The country is one of the world’s highest producers of the chemicals used to produce the synthetic opioid in America.

items.title: Who is Anna Lapwood, the content creator bringing organ music to millions on social media
items.summary: Anna Lapwood uses social media to share her love of the organ and favourite music from Bach to Lord of the Rings. Millions follow her and thousands more come to her concerts.

items.title: Road users encounter stray cattle on rural roads in the Western Australia's Great Southern.
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: More than 100 doctors file no-confidence vote in regional health service executive management
items.summary: Two top doctors and a nurse have left Albury Wodonga hospital in just six months, prompting a vote of no confidence in management.

items.title: Australian Medical Association speaks out after top doctor suspended from Albury Wodonga Health
items.summary: The Australian Medical Association is reviewing why several top doctors and nurses have left Albury Wodonga hospital.

items.title: Sheep stuck in flood waters
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Labor on track to win Nightcliff by-election, taking NT's single Greens seat
items.summary: Labor is on track to win the Nightcliff by-election, taking the Greens' first and only seat held in the Northern Territory parliament.

items.title: One Nation selects agricultural businessman David Farley to contest Farrer by-election in NSW
items.summary: David Farley, 69, defeats two other candidates in today's preselection battle at the Albury Entertainment Centre.

items.title: Teen photographer Casey Jacobs puts focus on life in rural WA
items.summary: Narrogin local Casey Jacobs is on a mission to capture agricultural landscapes across the Great Southern and share the stories of life on the land.

items.title: Man wins seatbelt fine court battle as AI traffic cameras face scrutiny
items.summary: A Queensland court decision casts fresh doubt on the reliability of controversial AI road-safety cameras after clearing a driver fined when his passenger moved their seatbelt during a trip.

items.title: Tasmanian nurses say lower wages, understaffing a barrier to returning home
items.summary: Tasmania needs more nurses, but lower wages and fewer opportunities for career growth remain barriers for enticing health workers to return to their home state.

items.title: Can the Coalition hold onto Sussan Ley’s former seat of Farrer?
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Nightcliff by-election will be a test for Territory Labor. Can it win it back?
items.summary: Territory Labor lost the previously safe seat of Nightcliff as it was bundled out of government. Eighteen months on, does it deserve to win the seat back?

items.title: Backlash deepens over AI seatbelt cameras as drivers fined for passengers’ behaviour
items.summary: Frustration is deepening over drivers being ordered to pay hundreds of dollars in fines for infringements incurred by their passengers — including young children and neurodivergent people not wearing seatbelts correctly.

items.title: Guide to Listening: ABC Classic's Festival of Female Composers and International Women's Day on ABC Jazz
items.summary: For 10 years, in the lead-up to International Women's Day, ABC Classic has celebrated classical music by women across centuries. Here's what's in store for 2026.

items.title: Lauren Parker to become dual Paralympian at Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Games
items.summary: Lauren Parker, who won gold as a triathlete at Paris 2024, will compete in two events in Milano Cortina 2026, less than a year after skiing for the first time.

items.title: Perth doctor Rhys Bellinge's 'entitled, callous' behaviour killed Elizabeth Pearce and ruined lives, court told
items.summary: Sought-after obstetrician Rhys Bellinge appeared to have it all — but that changed in an instant when his drunken, high-speed driving killed a young university graduate and left a rideshare driver with permanent injuries.

items.title: Disposable cameras in focus for Perth photography project
items.summary: A unique community exhibition in Perth is celebrating the "messiness and spontaneity" of the disposable camera.

items.title: CLP announces Anjan Paudel as Nightcliff by-election candidate, Phil Scott runs as independent
items.summary: More candidates for the Nightcliff by-election are announced, with Phil Scott running as an independent and the Country Liberal Party preselecting Anjan Paudel, who has stumbled while answering basic policy questions.

items.title: Geraldton's Horizon Ball sculpture draws large crowds as international visitor numbers rise
items.summary: Geraldton's Horizon Ball sculpture is drawing huge crowds of tourists after the spot gained international attention on social media.

items.title: Analogue camera technicians with decades of experience retiring amid boom in film photography
items.summary: As film photography surges in popularity among younger generations, technicians who repair analogue cameras are retiring, raising concerns it will become a dying art.

items.title: The making of Handel's Messiah into a centuries-old Christmas classic
items.summary: Composed in just three weeks, Handel’s Messiah remains a beloved classic nearly 300 years on. Its origin story is marked by scandal and links to slavery.

items.title: Labor faces green 'Grinch' hospital funding accusations with a budget in the red
items.summary: Labor has two options when it comes to straddling a budget in the red and accusations it has become a green Grinch on health funding.

items.title: Ashleigh Gentle shares 'incredibly isolating' miscarriage news before competing in T100 Qatar
items.summary: Australian triathlete Ashleigh Gentle reveals she suffered a miscarriage just days before competing in the season-ending T100 event in Qatar, earning significant praise for her bravery.

items.title: LNP's Wayde Chiesa promises to get to work after victory in Hinchinbrook by-election
items.summary: The LNP's Wayde Chiesa will be the next representative for the Queensland seat of Hinchinbrook, which had been safely held by Katter's Australian Party since 2017.

items.title: Former MP Nick Dametto declares victory in Townsville mayoral by-election
items.summary: Nick Dametto, a former Queensland MP, has won 61 per cent of the votes counted, succeeding in his campaign to become Townsville's next mayor.

items.title: Australian world champion Matthew Hauser wins Noosa Triathlon
items.summary: Only a fortnight after being crowned men's world champion, Australia's Matthew Hauser tops the podium at the revered Noosa Triathlon.

items.title: More accommodation desperately needed in Rockhampton and Yeppoon before 2032 Olympic Games
items.summary: More than half of Queensland has rental vacancy rates of less than 1 per cent, and experts say closed and abandoned properties may be the solution — especially with the 2032 Olympic Games looming.

items.title: Three youths charged with arson after fire at Ashley Youth Detention Centre in northern Tasmania
items.summary: Police said they were called to the site on Sunday afternoon following reports of a disturbance involving three armed youths.

items.title: Health, climate and community sport: What older voters in the Tasmanian seat of Bass are talking about this election
items.summary: The federal seat of Bass is held by a margin of just 1.4 per cent. For these older voters who live in the electorate, health, climate and community sport are the issues that will decide their vote.

items.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
items.summary: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

items.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
items.summary: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

items.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
items.summary: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

items.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
items.summary: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

items.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
items.summary: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.
